title: Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund
subtitle: The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.
caption:
Financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund

The total financial support provided to developing countries for climate change mitigation and adaptation. Countries have set a collective goal of mobilizing $100 billion per year from 2020 onwards.

The line chart tracks the total financial support provided through the Green Climate Fund from 2011 to 2022, measured in current US dollars per year. The data series begins in 2011 at a value just under $30 billion and remains relatively flat through 2012. A distinct upward shift occurs in 2013, where the value rises to approximately $40 billion. From 2013 to 2016, the trend continues to climb steadily, reaching slightly above $50 billion by 2016. The period between 2016 and 2020 shows some fluctuation; the value dips slightly in 2017, recovers to a local peak near $55 billion in 2018, and then settles back to roughly $52 billion in 2019 and 2020. Following 2020, the chart displays a sharp acceleration in growth. The line ascends steeply, crossing the $60 billion mark in 2021 and reaching its highest point in the series in 2022 at approximately $80 billion. Throughout the entire 12-year period shown, the annual financial support remains below the $100 billion collective goal mentioned in the subtitle, with the 2022 value representing the closest approach to that threshold within the visible timeframe.
